paragraph: The single-family market in Cresskill is competitive but not frenzied. Thirty homes sold over six months at a median of $1,012,000, with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.975 and a median of 29 days on market. About 30 percent of homes sold above asking, which means most sellers are pricing close to market and accepting offers near list. The range is wide, from $460,000 to $4,299,000, so positioning matters. Condos and townhouses move a bit faster at a median of 20 days on market and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.0, with 44 percent selling above asking. Nine units sold at a median of $895,000 over the same period. If you're comparing the two tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home or the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo or townhouse.
